dataset: synthetic NewsItem instances (unique source_id per trial for ingest, one repeated item for dedup), and synthetic headlines scored via DeterministicSentimentScorer.score_batch in one batched call -- 10,000 trials each
source: tests/nlp/test_performance.py's three scenarios, re-run standalone with resource.getrusage wrapped around the same loops
known_gaps: FinBertSentimentScorer's real inference latency is deliberately not benchmarked here -- torch/transformers are not installed by the base dev extras (see ADR-018's pyproject.toml note), and its cost is dominated by model inference (softmax over a transformer forward pass), not this package's own logic. batch_sentiment_scoring_ms_per_headline measures DeterministicSentimentScorer only, a dict lookup per headline.

ingest_ms_per_call and dedup_check_ms_per_call measure InMemoryNewsItemStore only -- JsonlNewsItemStore's file I/O is deliberately not benchmarked, matching Milestone 9's JsonlExperienceStore precedent.

memory_mb (29.77) is consistent with memory's ~34.65mb (v0.9-memory-loop.json) for the same reason: nlp's Phase A/C code has no transitive third-party dependency of its own (torch/transformers are only imported lazily inside FinBertSentimentScorer, never exercised in this benchmark).
